title: 1.3: Reports the number of CAN frames generated for 20-byte payload (e.g., 3 frames total, 1 First Frame plus 2 Consecutive Frames, three frame sequence)
1.4: Provides runtime execution evidence for 20-byte payload fragmentation (e.g., shows actual frame hex data, displays command output with frame bytes, demonstrates executed test results)
1.5: Describes the first byte format of First Frame (e.g., 0x10 pattern, upper nibble 0x1, byte value 16 decimal)
1.6: Describes the first byte format of Consecutive Frames (e.g., 0x2X pattern with sequence number, upper nibble 0x2 with incrementing lower nibble, values like 0x21 then 0x22)
1.7: Reports the maximum single-frame payload size (e.g., 7 bytes maximum, payloads up to 7 bytes use single frame, 8 bytes triggers segmentation)
1.8: Reports that 5000-byte payload triggers extended length encoding (e.g., uses 32-bit length field, employs ISO 15765-2:2015 format, exceeds 12-bit limit)

1.9: Explains what error would occur for payloads exceeding the limit (e.g., "Too much data in ISOTP message" for payloads beyond 4GB, Scapy_Exception at maximum size, identifies when fragmentation fails)
1.10: Reports the timeout value for missing consecutive frames (e.g., 1 second timeout, cf_timeout value, receiver timeout period)
1.11: Identifies the warning message for missing frame timeout (e.g., "RX state was reset due to timeout", timeout warning log, receiver reset message)
